Metadata.title: BUILD4VENEZUELA | Global Hackathon
Metadata.description: A global 72-hour hackathon to build useful tools for Venezuela after the June 24 earthquakes.

BrandPage.title: Share the signal.
BrandPage.description: Use these official Build4Venezuela assets, fonts, and ready-to-post images to invite more builders, designers, volunteers, and allies into the effort.

brief.title: Make the tools reach Venezuela.
brief.description: The goal is not to make the technology look impressive. The goal is for every affected person, family member, or volunteer to know that useful tools already exist and where to find them.
brief.message: Hundreds of people are building tools after the earthquakes. They only help if they reach the people who need them. Share, tag, and help the signal travel inside Venezuela.
checklist: Mention one concrete tool and what it helps with.
Verify the project link and status at build4venezuela.com/projects.
Tag Venezuelan creators or community accounts that can amplify.
Keep the tone respectful: hope, not spectacle.

links.label: Projects
links.text: Share the directory of tools that are live or in progress.

links.label: Brand kit
links.text: Use ready-to-post visuals and official campaign assets.

links.label: WhatsApp
links.text: Send useful tools into informal community groups.

logo.title: Build4Venezuela Logo
logo.description: Primary campaign logo in SVG format.

mark.title: V Mark
mark.description: Compact mark for avatars, badges, and small placements.

map.title: Venezuela Map
map.description: Map illustration used across the campaign visuals.

leftHand.title: Left Hand
leftHand.description: Left-side hand illustration for poster and hero compositions.

rightHand.title: Right Hand
rightHand.description: Right-side hand illustration for poster and hero compositions.

fonts.title: Input Mono Narrow
fonts.note: Use the campaign font for posters, captions, slides, and project updates when possible. Keep messages urgent, useful, and easy to read.

social.title: Ready to post
social.description: Download an image, post it on your social channels, and ask people to join, build, translate, mentor, donate, or share verified information.

portrait.title: Portrait Post
portrait.description: Tall feed image for Instagram and LinkedIn.

story.title: Story
story.description: Vertical story format for Instagram, TikTok, and WhatsApp.

hero.title: build tech solutions for Venezuela.

labels.complete: The closure event has started. Keep building.

hero.description: The formal event is closing, but the work continues: keep improving projects, contribute to existing ones, or propose what Venezuela still needs.

context.title: Build beyond one week.
context.firstParagraph: The earthquakes left problems that need help now and will not be solved in one week. Families, volunteers, organizations, and communities still need reliable information, coordination, access to aid, and tools that work with limited connectivity.
context.secondParagraph: Build4Venezuela is moving from a sprint into a longer collective effort: short-term support for the emergency, and long-term solutions that help Venezuela recover, coordinate, and grow stronger.
context.latestInfoQuery: What's the latest on the Venezuela earthquakes?

impactStats.label: propose a new problem to solve
design for recovery, not only response

principles.title: Trust is the operating system.
principles.description: These principles guide code, communication, coordination, design decisions, group messages, and media interactions. If a decision contradicts them, revisit it before moving forward.

items.title: People first
items.text: Build for someone in Venezuela with low signal, low battery, and urgent needs, not for demos.

items.title: No duplication
items.text: Before building something new, ask what already exists and how to connect with it.

items.title: Long-term trust
items.text: Be transparent about what works, what does not, and what each tool can realistically do.

items.title: Do no harm
items.text: Respect rescue teams and specialized organizations. Do not amplify unverified rescue information or saturate emergency channels.

filter.title: Before publishing or building
items: Does this serve a real person in Venezuela right now, or just make us feel useful?
Are we duplicating something that already exists, or can we coordinate?
Does this create trust, or does it create confusion?
Could this interfere with rescue work or specialized emergency teams?

projects.title: What you can build
projects.description: Choose a concrete problem. Connect with what already exists. Leave something useful for the emergency and durable enough for recovery.

projectIdeas: Verified directories for shelters, collection centers, and medical care.
Lightweight maps to report needs by area and update statuses.
WhatsApp bots to guide families, volunteers, and donors.
Tools to coordinate volunteers, transportation, inventory, and deliveries.
Simple pages to translate critical information and fight rumors.
Tracking systems for cases, aid requests, and local contacts.

channels.label: Luma
channels.text: Join the closure event and receive official updates.

channels.label: WhatsApp
channels.text: Join the group for fast coordination and urgent announcements.

channels.label: Discord
channels.text: Find a team, continue a project, propose a new one, and coordinate support.

channels.label: GitHub
channels.text: Review the repository, open issues, and share project progress.

footer.description: Build for Venezuela is organized by a community of builders, designers, and allies who believe technology can also be a form of solidarity.

grid.empty: No projects yet. Be the first one to add a build.

personas.label: Find People
personas.title: Missing & Found People
personas.description: Report, search, and reunite missing people and pets — WhatsApp/Telegram bots, facial recognition, and rescue maps. Join here if you want to help families find each other.

recursos.label: Aid Coordination
recursos.title: Aid & Resource Coordination
recursos.description: Live maps and directories of shelters, collection centers, hospitals, volunteers, and needs by zone. Join here to help route help to where it's needed.

data.label: Data & APIs
data.title: Data, Dedup & Shared APIs
data.description: The shared data layer — scraping, normalization, deduplication, and open APIs other tools build on. Join here to make every other tool speak the same truth.

damage.label: Damage Assessment
damage.title: Structural Damage Assessment
damage.description: Crowdsourced and AI-assisted assessment of building damage, habitability, and structural risk. Join here if you work with engineering, CV, or satellite data.

donations.label: Donations
donations.title: Donations & Funding
donations.description: Crowdfunding, donor matching, and transparent aid payments. Join here to move money and supplies to people who need them.

infra.label: Connectivity & Infra
infra.title: Connectivity & Infrastructure
infra.description: Offline mesh networking, edge inference, sensing, and alerts that keep tools running when the grid is down. Join here for the hardest, highest-leverage bets.

info.label: Verified Info
info.title: Verified Info & Anti-misinformation
info.description: Fighting rumors with verified, real-time information. Join here to keep the public informed and the noise down.

other.label: Other
other.title: Community & Other
other.description: Community, meta, and builds that don't fit a single cluster yet.

SubmitProject.title: Add your project
SubmitProject.description: Every accepted project gets a permanent page at /p/your-slug. You can edit the slug later from this browser.

EditProject.title: Update your project
EditProject.description: Change any field, including the slug. The page URL updates after saving.

## What did you build?

Who is it for? How does it help? What should people try first?

Comments.title: Build feedback
Comments.placeholder: Add a useful question, note, or suggestion...
Comments.signedOutDescription: Sign in to leave feedback and vote on comments.
Comments.empty: No comments yet. Start the thread with a sharp question or useful suggestion.

metadata.title: Requests for solutions | Build4Venezuela
metadata.description: A live board where people in Venezuela can request useful solutions, vote, and discuss what builders should make next.

Requests.title: Tell builders what matters.
Requests.description: If you are in Venezuela, leave concrete problems that technology, coordination, logistics, or information tools could help solve. The community votes and comments in real time.

board.formDescription: Name the problem. Add context only if it helps builders understand who is affected, what fails today, and what a good outcome looks like.
board.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional markdown: who needs this, what is hard today, links, constraints...
board.signedOutDescription: Sign in to leave ideas, vote, and comment.
board.noDescription: No extra description yet. Use the comments to ask for missing context.
board.commentPlaceholder: Ask a question, confirm the need, or add context...
board.empty: No requests yet. Start with one concrete problem builders should solve.

deck.title: Build4Venezuela Deck
deck.description: Build4Venezuela project presentation in Google Slides.

brand.title: Brand and sharing brief
brand.description: Official assets, posting guidance, and links to help the tools reach Venezuela.
